title: Dry Crunchy Algerian Vegetable Tagine
description: A bitter and crunchy vegetable dish originating from Algeria, featuring unflavored gelatin and cooked in a ttukbaegi earthenware pot, also known as a cazuela, this recipe is jain friendly.

ingredients.name: carrots
ingredients.notes: peeled and cut into 1 inch sticks

instructions: Preheat the oven to 300 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a small bowl, dissolve the gelatin in water and set aside.
In a large cazuela or earthenware pot, heat the olive oil over medium heat.
Add the onions and garlic and cook until the onions are translucent.
Add the eggplant, zucchini, carrots, and bell peppers, and cook until the vegetables are tender.
Add the dissolved gelatin, salt, and black pepper, and stir to combine.
Transfer the pot to the preheated oven and cook for 20 minutes.
Remove the pot from the oven and let it cool to room temperature.
Once cooled, refrigerate the tagine for at least 2 hours to allow it to set.
Before serving, cut the tagine into small pieces and serve chilled.
